---
title: "Exercise 03 — R Testing with testthat"
format: html
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note}
🇫🇷 [Version française](../../../exercises/03_r/README.qmd)
:::

## Objective

Test an R function that computes per-group statistics on a TSV file.

## Quick Reference — testthat

Documentation: <https://testthat.r-lib.org/>

| Element | Syntax |
|---------|---------|
| Equality | `expect_equal(a, b)` |
| Expected error | `expect_error(expr)` |
| Match on message | `expect_error(expr, "pattern")` |
| True / false | `expect_true(expr)` / `expect_false(expr)` |

---

## Context

The `compute_stats()` function reads a data frame with columns `id`, `group`, `value`
and returns the mean and standard deviation per group.

## Starting Code

`training/pipelines/minimal/scripts/stats_records.R`:

```r
load_records <- function(path) {
    if (!file.exists(path)) stop(paste("File not found:", path))
    df <- read.table(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    if (nrow(df) == 0) stop("File has no data rows")
    df
}

compute_stats <- function(df) {
    stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))
    required <- c("id", "group", "value")
    missing <- setdiff(required, names(df))
    if (length(missing) > 0) stop(paste("Missing columns:", paste(missing, collapse = ", ")))

    groups <- unique(df$group)
    data.frame(
        group = groups,
        mean  = sapply(groups, function(g) mean(df$value[df$group == g])),
        sd    = sapply(groups, function(g) sd(df$value[df$group == g])),
        row.names = NULL
    )
}
```

## Exercise

Complete `tests/testthat/test_stats_records.R`:

```r
library(testthat)
source("training/pipelines/minimal/scripts/stats_records.R")

make_records <- function() {
  # TODO : créer un petit data.frame avec colonnes id, group, value
}

test_that("résultat est un data.frame avec les bonnes colonnes", {
  # TODO
})

test_that("moyenne par groupe calculée correctement", {
  # TODO : vérifier la moyenne du groupe A et du groupe B
})

test_that("toutes les moyennes sont non négatives", {
  # TODO
})

test_that("erreur si colonne manquante", {
  # TODO
})
```

## Expected Result

```
[ FAIL 0 | WARN 0 | SKIP 0 | PASS 5 ]
```

## Solution

→ See [solution.qmd](solution.qmd)
